calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact; For Declatory Relief; And For Injunctive Relief
Off Calendar May-26-2010 Off Calendar

PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact; For Declatory Relief; And For Injunctive Relief
Off Calendar Feb-24-2010 Continued To May-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon O'Donnell for Wells Fargo Bank. Robert Rothman for Diana Boddie (Respondent). John Boessenecker for and with Elicia Phillips (Petitioner).

The Parties reported that they reached further stipulation and read the terms for the record. The Court stated that it had an issue with ordering title of the property and that there was nothing before the Court to have basis to order that the property is part of the Trust. Mr. O'Donnell stated that he did not have authority to speak on behalf of First American Loanstar with regard to the terms that had been stated and that he would contact them. All Parties agreed to extending the injunction and enjoin in the sale of the real property for a period of four months from the date of signing of the formal order with regard to the terms of the stipulation. The matter was continued to May 26, 2010 for status of the sale of the property.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact; For Declatory Relief; And For Injunctive Relief
Off Calendar Jan-27-2010 Continued To Feb-24-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact; For Declatory Relief; And For Injunctive Relief
Off Calendar Dec-09-2009 Continued To Jan-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Rothman for Diana Boddie. John Boessenecker for and with Elica Phillips.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to jurisdiction, stated that they were in agreement to sell the property, to continue the restraint on Wells Fargo with regard to the foreclosure and that the only issue was with choosing a Broker/Real Estate Agent. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation and stipulated to allowing the Mediator to choose the Broker/Real Estate Agent to list and sell the real property located at 127-129 Hugo St. in San Francisco. Mediation is to be completed by January 20, 2010. Status of mediation was set for January 27, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 (order for the same signed in open court). The matter was also continued to that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
